skill.name: google-agents-cli-publish
skill.description: Guides users through publishing agents to Gemini Enterprise and managing the Agent Registry using the agents-cli publish gemini-enterprise command. Covers ADK and A2A registration modes, programmatic and interactive usage, flag reference, auto-detection from deployment metadata, Agent Registry fleet management, and troubleshooting. Part of the Google ADK skills suite.
skill.summary: Publish and register agents to Gemini Enterprise, then manage them in the Agent Registry using agents-cli commands.
skill.author: google

security_audit.summary: All 108 static analysis findings are false positives. The file is a markdown documentation file (SKILL.md) containing bash command examples for Google's agents-cli tool. The scanner misinterpreted markdown backtick code fences as Ruby shell execution, the word 'description' as a weak cryptographic algorithm reference, example placeholder URLs as network threats, and example GCP resource IDs as system reconnaissance. The skill contains no executable code, no prompt injection attempts, no data exfiltration patterns, and no obfuscated content. It is a legitimate Google-authored skill for publishing agents to Gemini Enterprise.

content.user_title: Publish Agents to Gemini Enterprise with agents-cli
content.value_statement: Publishing agents to Gemini Enterprise requires knowing the correct flags, registration modes, and authentication setup. This skill provides step-by-step guidance for ADK and A2A registration, programmatic CI/CD usage, and Agent Registry management.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through ADK agent registration to Gemini Enterprise with the correct flags and resource names
Guide users through A2A agent registration with agent card URLs and deployment targets
Provide correct flags and environment variables for non-interactive CI/CD pipeline publishing
Explain auto-detection of agent runtime ID and registration type from deployment metadata
Walk through Agent Registry fleet management with gcloud commands for listing and updating agents
Troubleshoot common registration errors including SDK version issues and permission errors
limitations: Does not deploy agents to Cloud Run or Agent Runtime - use the google-agents-cli-deploy skill for that
Does not create Gemini Enterprise apps - those must already exist in Google Cloud Console before registration
Agent Registry functionality is in Preview and gcloud commands may change with future releases
Cannot help with Gemini Enterprise app creation or configuration outside of agent registration

use_cases.title: Publish an ADK Agent to Gemini Enterprise
use_cases.description: A developer who deployed an ADK agent needs to register it with Gemini Enterprise so end users can access it through the Gemini Enterprise app.

use_cases.title: Automate Publishing in CI/CD Pipelines
use_cases.description: A platform engineer wants to add agent publishing to an automated CI/CD pipeline using environment variables and non-interactive flags for agent registration.

use_cases.title: Manage the Agent Registry Fleet
use_cases.description: A cloud administrator needs to list all registered agents, inspect their details, update metadata, and delete outdated agents across multiple projects.

prompt_templates.title: Publish a deployed ADK agent
prompt_templates.prompt: I deployed my ADK agent with agents-cli deploy and have the deployment_metadata.json file. Help me publish it to Gemini Enterprise.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user who deployed an agent and needs to register it with Gemini Enterprise

prompt_templates.title: Register an A2A agent
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an A2A agent running on Cloud Run with the URL https://my-service-abc123.us-east1.run.app. Help me register it with Gemini Enterprise.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with an A2A-compatible agent who needs to publish it to Gemini Enterprise

prompt_templates.title: Set up CI/CD publishing
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to add agent publishing to my GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline. Show me which environment variables and flags to use for non-interactive publishing.
prompt_templates.scenario: DevOps engineer integrating agent publishing into an automated pipeline

prompt_templates.title: List and manage registered agents
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to see all my registered agents, update their display names, and delete old ones. Walk me through the Agent Registry gcloud commands.
prompt_templates.scenario: Administrator managing multiple published agents across projects

output_examples.input: I published my agent but users get a Session not found error when they try to use it.
output_examples.output: This error is caused by an outdated google-cloud-aiplatform SDK version. Versions 1.128.0 and older have this bug. Run 'pip install --upgrade google-cloud-aiplatform' to upgrade the SDK, then redeploy the agent with 'agents-cli deploy' and re-register it with 'agents-cli publish gemini-enterprise'. Registration is idempotent so re-running it updates the existing entry.

output_examples.input: How do I publish an agent without interactive prompts for my CI/CD pipeline?
output_examples.output: Use non-interactive mode by providing all required values as flags or environment variables. For example, set AGENT_RUNTIME_ID, GEMINI_ENTERPRISE_APP_ID, and GEMINI_DISPLAY_NAME environment variables, then run 'agents-cli publish gemini-enterprise'. Use REGISTRATION_TYPE=adk or REGISTRATION_TYPE=a2a depending on your agent type. The --interactive flag should not be used in automated pipelines.

best_practices: Always include deployment_metadata.json from agents-cli deploy so that agent runtime ID and registration type are auto-detected
Use environment variables instead of inline flags in CI/CD pipelines to keep sensitive resource names out of command logs
Upgrade google-cloud-aiplatform SDK before registering to avoid Session not found errors
anti_patterns: Registering an agent before it is deployed and reachable, which causes registration to fail with connection errors
Using --interactive mode in automated CI/CD pipelines, which causes the command to hang waiting for input
Manually specifying flags that could be auto-detected from deployment_metadata.json, increasing the chance of typos and mismatches

faq.question: What is the difference between ADK and A2A registration?
faq.answer: ADK registration is for standard ADK agents deployed to Agent Runtime and registers the agent directly via its reasoning engine resource name. A2A registration is for agents using the Agent-to-Agent protocol and requires an agent card URL that the command fetches to register.

faq.question: Can I publish an agent without deployment_metadata.json?
faq.answer: Yes, but you must provide all required flags manually including --agent-runtime-id or --agent-card-url, --registration-type, and --gemini-enterprise-app-id. The metadata file enables auto-detection of these values.

faq.question: Is publishing idempotent? Can I re-publish the same agent?
faq.answer: Yes, registration is idempotent. Re-running the publish command for an already registered agent updates the existing registration in place instead of creating a duplicate.

faq.question: What permissions do I need to publish an agent?
faq.answer: You need Discovery Engine Editor permissions on the Gemini Enterprise project. For A2A on Cloud Run, the Discovery Engine service account also needs roles/run.servicesInvoker on the Cloud Run service.

faq.question: What causes the Session not found error after registration?
faq.answer: This is caused by an outdated google-cloud-aiplatform SDK version 1.128.0 or older. Upgrade the SDK, redeploy the agent, and re-register to resolve the issue.

faq.question: How do I see all my registered agents?
faq.answer: Use the gcloud alpha agent-registry agents list command with your project and location. You can filter by name using the --filter flag, for example --filter='displayName:my-agent'.
